title: Quadlet does not support multiple argument command
body:
### Issue Description

Using the new Quadlet tool it seems impossible to create a systemd service with multiple arguments in the command. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create a file at `~/.config/containers/systemd/python.container` with the following content:
```ini
[Unit]
Description=Python container

# Specify the dependencies
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target nss-lookup.target

[Container]
Image=docker.io/python:3.11
Exec='python'
Exec='-m'
Exec='http.server'

# Enable auto-update container
AutoUpdate=registry

PublishPort=8000:8000

Timezone=local

[Service]
Restart=on-failure

# Extend Timeout to allow time to pull the image
TimeoutStartSec=300

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```
2. Run `systemctl --user daemon-reload`
3. Run `systemctl start --user python.service`



### Describe the results you received

The service fails to start

### Describe the results you expected

The service start successfully 

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Tested on Archlinux (v4.9.3) and Debian Testing (v4.9.3)

### Additional information

After quick check it seems to me that the generator does not support this behavior.
Running `systemctl cat --user python.service` gives the following
<details>
<summary>systemctl cat result</summary>

```ini
# /run/user/1000/systemd/generator/python.service
# Automatically generated by /usr/lib/systemd/user-generators/podman-user-generator
# 
[Unit]
Description=Python container

# Specify the dependencies
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target nss-lookup.target
SourcePath=/home/user/.config/containers/systemd/python.container
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[X-Container]
Image=docker.io/python:3.11
Exec='python'
Exec='-m'
Exec='http.server'

# Enable auto-update container
AutoUpdate=registry

PublishPort=8000:8000

Timezone=local

[Service]
Restart=on-failure

# Extend Timeout to allow time to pull the image
TimeoutStartSec=300
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
KillMode=mixed
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
ExecStopPost=-/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
Delegate=yes
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all
SyslogIdentifier=%N
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name=systemd-%N --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --tz=local --sdnotify=conmon -d --label io.containers.autoupdate=registry --publish 8000:8000 docker.io/python:3.11 http.server

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```
</details>

Looking at `ExecStart=` line it seems that `podman-user-generator` only keep the latest `Exec` value instead of concatenating them

comments.body:
@zephvr thanks opening this issue.

For your example, you need to put all the `Exec` values together:
```
Exec=python -m http.server
```
Is there a reason why splitting the `Exec` values into multiple line is required?
